channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: 10 Healthy Nigerian Foods That Can Support Weight Loss When Prepared Right
item.description: The article explains say losing weight for Nigeria no mean say you must abandon local food. Instead, e dey focus on how Nigerian meals fit support weight loss when dem prepare am well, reduce oil, and...

item.title: Rising internal tensions in Lebanon revive fears of another civil war amid ongoing conflict
item.description: The article explains how many Lebanese citizens are increasingly worried that their country may slide back into civil war, as current tensions resemble those seen during the 1975–1990 conflict. Follow...

item.title: King Charles receives Earth Day environmental award for long-standing sustainability advocacy
item.description: King Charles has been recognised with an environmental award in conjunction with World Earth Day, acknowledging his longstanding commitment to environmental protection and sustainability. The recognit...

item.title: Former MrBeast employee sues over alleged sexual harassment and unfair dismissal after maternity leave
item.description: A former Beast Industries employee, Lorrayne Mavromatis, has filed a federal lawsuit in North Carolina against MrBeastYouTube, LLC and GameChanger 24/7, LLC, alleging years of sexual harassment and ge...

item.title: Zach Galifianakis brings humour and accessibility to beginner gardening in Netflix’s new series
item.description: This review discusses This Is a Gardening Show, a six-part Netflix series hosted by comedian Zach Galifianakis, highlighting its appeal as an engaging and accessible introduction to gardening. Unlike ...

item.title: House of Reps Passes First Reading of Cybercrime Amendment Bill to Protect Journalists
item.description: Na so di House of Representatives for Nigeria on Wednesday, April 22, 2026, pass di first reading of one important bill to amend di Cybercrime (Prohibition, Prevention, Etc) Act 2015. Di bill wey dem ...

item.title: Cocaine Pollution Alters Wild Salmon Movement and Behavior, Study Finds
item.description: A recent scientific study has provided new evidence that cocaine pollution in aquatic environments can significantly alter the behavior of wild fish. Researchers from several international institution...

item.title: Manchester City edge Burnley 1–0 to go top of Premier League and confirm Burnley’s relegation
item.description: Manchester City moved to the top of the Premier League table after a hard‑fought 1–0 victory over Burnley at Turf Moor on Wednesday night. Erling Haaland scored the only goal in the fifth minute, capi...

item.title: HESLB and Posta sign agreement to improve student loan application services and reduce documentation challenges
item.description: The Higher Education Students’ Loans Board (HESLB) and the Tanzania Posts Corporation (Posta) have entered into a new cooperation agreement aimed at making the student loan application process easier ...

item.title: Food vlogger Ninong Ry announces he is expecting his third child
item.description: Popular Filipino food vlogger and content creator Ninong Ry, known for his fun and relatable cooking videos, is set to become a father for the third time. According to the latest entertainment news fr...

item.title: Air-conditioning technician arrested in Cagayan de Oro mall after making bomb joke to security guard
item.description: A 28-year-old air-conditioning technician was arrested in Cagayan de Oro City on Tuesday afternoon after he jokingly told a female security guard that he was carrying a bomb inside his backpack. The i...

item.title: Kelvin Miranda opens up about grieving his father while continuing his runway commitments
item.description: Actor Kelvin Miranda shared his personal struggle with grief following the passing of his father, revealing how it affected him even in the middle of his professional commitments. In an exclusive inte...

item.title: Rural West Bengal elections shaped by local presence, governance networks and voter perception
item.description: The article examines how electoral dynamics in rural and riverine parts of West Bengal are strongly influenced by a party’s ground presence and its ability to address local needs, rather than broader ...

item.title: Political dynasties persist despite doubts of patriarchs in Marcos and Duterte families
item.description: The article reflects on the continuing dominance of political dynasties in the Philippines, focusing on the Marcos and Duterte families. It highlights how both Ferdinand Marcos Sr. and Rodrigo Duterte...

item.title: Violence in southern Lebanon undermines ceasefire ahead of US-hosted talks in Washington
item.description: Tensions have escalated again in southern Lebanon after renewed clashes between Israel and Hezbollah, putting a fragile, US-mediated ceasefire under serious strain just before key diplomatic talks in ...

item.title: Social media campaigning continues during election silence period despite restrictions in Tamil Nadu
item.description: The article reports on how political campaigning continued in Tamil Nadu during the official silence period ahead of elections, despite strict legal prohibitions. According to the Representation of th...

item.title: Nearly complete Alnashetri fossil sheds new light on alvarezsaur evolution
item.description: A newly analyzed fossil of Alnashetri cerropoliciensis, a small dinosaur discovered in Patagonia, is helping scientists better understand the evolution of alvarezsaurs—a group of unusual theropods kno...

item.title: Gibraltar macaques eat soil to ease stomach irritation from tourist junk food, study finds
item.description: Researchers have observed that Barbary macaques living on the Rock of Gibraltar are eating soil to help ease digestive discomfort caused by human junk food left behind by tourists. The monkeys, which ...

item.title: US explores third-country resettlement options for stranded Afghan evacuees amid visa delays
item.description: The United States is reported to be in discussions with the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) to resettle around 1,100 Afghan nationals who are currently stranded in Qatar. These individuals had earl...

item.title: Call for Strong Enforcement Against Illegal Roadside Fuel Sales in Tanzania
item.description: This editorial highlights the growing concern over illegal roadside fuel vending along highways in Tanzania, describing it as a serious threat to both public safety and the national economy. Fuel is i...
